title: Podman kube generate creates invalid volume names when dots are included in the path
body:
### Issue Description

When using `podman kube generate mypod` to create a kubernetes style yaml of a running pod, the generated yaml is invalid according to kubectl:

```
* spec.volumes[1].name: Invalid value: "{...}/example.com/{....}": must not contain dots
```

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Create a pod
2. Add a container with a volume where the host path contains a dot (like the above `/example.com/`)
3. Try to `kubectl apply` the yaml

### Describe the results you received

Pod not being applied due to the above error message

### Describe the results you expected

"pod/mypod created"

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless, but i doubt it matters for this issue

### Upstream Latest Release

Latest fedora provided release

### Additional environment details

N/A

### Additional information

After manually replacing the dots with `-` within the generated yaml, the pod started fine on my "kind" local cluster.

comments.body:
Maybe for reference, extracted from the generated yaml and redacted for privacy:

```yaml
 - hostPath:
      path: /home/theseer/Desktop/example.com/web/nginx/proxy.conf
      type: File
    name: home-theseer-desktop-example.com-web-nginx-proxy.conf-host-7
```

Also holds true for container names btw:

```yaml
...
spec:
  containers:
  - args:
    - nginx
    - -g
    - daemon off;
    image: docker.io/library/nginx:alpine
    name: web.nginx
....
```
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
